title: DID document uses zeroed placeholder key when recovery_pubkey is NULL
description: In src/activitypub.rs lines 830-835, when an account has no recovery_pubkey (NULL in DB), the DID document endpoint uses `[0u8; 32]` as a placeholder for the Ed25519 public key. This publishes a DID document with a meaningless zeroed verification method that nobody can authenticate with. The document should either omit the recovery-key verification method entirely when recovery_pubkey is NULL, or return a 404/503 with a message to run backfill first. Publishing a zeroed key is worse than publishing no key -- it suggests a valid verification method exists when it does not.
